task: Write a Python function longest_word(s) returning the longest whitespace-separated word in the string. On ties return the last such word, not the first. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all the criteria, including correctly handling an empty string.
trace: I followed the given criteria by ensuring the function is correct and concise. The approach involves splitting the input string into words, iterating through them from last to first (to handle ties by returning the last occurrence), and updating the longest word accordingly. The edge case where the input string is empty is explicitly handled by returning an empty string immediately.
